name: Faria
tagline: Oahu's only Portuguese restaurant — 'Pacifika Portuguese' from a chef honoring Hawaii's deep Lusitanian roots.
mustOrder: any daily fish preparation — chef Kawehi Haug sources local
Portuguese sausage dishes — connecting Hawaii's beloved breakfast staple to its origins
desserts with tropical twists on Portuguese classics
talkStory: Portuguese immigrants came to Hawaii in the late 1800s to work sugar plantations and left a permanent mark — malasadas, Portuguese sausage, sweet bread, and bean soup are all local staples. Yet until Faria opened in January 2025, there was no dedicated Portuguese restaurant on Oahu. Chef-owner Kawehi Haug calls the cuisine 'Pacifika Portuguese,' bridging traditional recipes with local Hawaiian ingredients. The intimate Kailua space fills fast; reservations are essential. If you've ever wondered where malasadas actually came from, this is the deeper story on a plate.
